ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.
text: Ŋyunti ŋun ní tiwaŋkuti tin yáàyee yaa làá jɔ̄m̀ tɔbee, ní Jaakɔb ń yòon̄ní ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee kí ŋá tiwan tin poon tiwaŋkuti nyùee kí túĺ ti-nimbiin, ní tì jɔ́ḿ tɔb ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee cee.
translated: And Jacob lived in the land of Ham, which is the father of Canaan, and saw the daughters of Shechem, and took a wife from the daughters of Shechem, and went in to her.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Ní Jaakɔb mun nín nìkì u-saŋŋu. U cée ní Unimbɔti tuuŋyi cílìń u-pu nsanni.
translated: And Jacob went to the land of the easterners and lived there and worked as a hired hand for a man named Laban

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.

translated: When the Unimbɔti people go to a place, they go to a place of their ancestors, and their ancestors also go to the Unimbɔti people's place.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.

translated: When the moon is full, the women go to the farm, when the moon is full, the men go to the farm, when the moon is full, the children go to the farm to help their parents, that is why the moon is important to us.

translated: Abraham took his son, and also the donkey and the servant, and they went to the mountain that God had told him, and on the third day he saw the mountain from a distance.

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.
text: Ŋyunti ŋun ní tiwaŋkuti tin yáàyee yaa làá jɔ̄m̀ tɔbee, ní Jaakɔb ń yòon̄ní ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee kí ŋá tiwan tin poon tiwaŋkuti nyùee kí túĺ ti-nimbiin, ní tì jɔ́ḿ tɔb ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee cee.
translated: And Jacob lived in the land of his father's sojournings, in the land of Canaan, and this is the history of Jacob, and Joseph whom his father loved more than his brothers.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Ní Jaakɔb mun nín nìkì u-saŋŋu. U cée ní Unimbɔti tuuŋyi cílìń u-pu nsanni.
translated: And Jacob took a stone and put it under his head and he slept and had a dream
